messages.content:
Put the verb into the correct form:
They could go on vacation next year if they ____ (save) money.
They could go on vacation next year if they save money.

Put the words into the correct order:
she / schools. / prime minister, / would / in / more money / was / invest / she / If
If she was prime minister, she would invest more money in schools.

Put the verb into the correct form:
If you ____ (not / hurry), you will miss the train.
If you do not hurry, you will miss the train.

Put the verb into the correct form:
I would have gone to Span if I ____ (get) a well-paid job last year.
I would have gone to Spain if I had gotten a well-paid job last year.

Put the words in the correct order:
more / had / you / If you / would / a dog, / exercise. / get
If you had a dog, you would get more exercise.

Put the verbs into the correct form:
If you ____ (pass) a chemist's, ____ (you / get) me some cough medicine? - Yes, certainly.
If you pass a chemist's, will you get me some cough medicine? - Yes, certainly.

Put the words in the correct order:
I were you, / would not miss / to become a businessman! / I / the chance / If
If I were you, I would not miss the chance to become a businessman!

Put the words in the correct order:
arrive, / the bus / walk home. / If / will / we / does not
If the bus does not arrive, we will walk home.

Put the verb into the correct form:
If only Stuart ____ (call) me. - Don't worry. I'm sure he'll phone soon.
If only Stuart would call me. - Don't worry. I'm sure he'll phone soon.

Put the verb into the correct form:
If the team had played harder, we ____ (not / lose) the match last week.
If the team had played harder, we would not have lost the match last week.

Put the verb into the correct form:
If only the wind ____ (stop) blowing so hard. - Yes, it's very windy today, isn't it?
If only the wind would stop blowing so hard. - Yes, it's very windy today, isn't it?

Choose the correct option to complete the sentence:
If I had known about the meeting, I _____ it.

a.
would have attended

b.
will attend

c.
attended
a. would have attended

Put the verbs into the correct form:
The financial situation is critical, and if we ____ (not / act) now, it ____ (be) too late.
The financial situation is critical, and if we do not act now, it will be too late.
